summary: This cluster of research centers on the industrial cultivation and commercialization of three key microalgae species: *Chlorella vulgaris*, *Spirulina platensis*, and *Haematococcus pluvialis*. The studies collectively traced the historical evolution of microalgae culture from fundamental natural science into applied biotechnology, with a primary focus on optimizing production methods for nutraceuticals, cosmetics, and biofuel applications. A consistent pattern across the cluster was the emphasis on developing scalable cultivation strategies and commercialization pathways to transform these species into high-value industrial resources. The geographic and ecological scope was broad, reflecting global efforts to standardize and expand microalgae-based industries.
